triage.impactDetail: Customer-facing checkout flow is down. Estimated 100% of purchase attempts failing since 10:15 UTC. Approximately 15,000 users affected.
triage.compoundScenario: Scenario 4: Retry Storm (transient network delay ⇒ client retries flood ⇒ server overwhelmed)

metadata.logTemplate: FATAL: DB connection pool exhausted. {*} active connections available.
metadata.rawLogSample: 2026-04-18 10:15:30 FATAL: DB connection pool exhausted. 0 active connections available.

hypotheses.title: DB connection pool exhaustion after pool size reduction
hypotheses.reasoning: Active connections hit configured limit immediately after deploy reduced pool_size. Latency spike correlates with saturation onset.

hypotheses.title: Config regression in latest deployment
hypotheses.reasoning: Deploy at 10:14 changed db_pool_size from 200 to 100. Time correlation is strong but not exclusive.

hypotheses.title: Downstream dependency timeout cascade
hypotheses.reasoning: Recommendation-service latency rose 3x, potentially holding DB connections longer.

evidence.text: DB active connections reached configured limit of 100
Connection timeout errors increased 50x in 5 minutes
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 10:15:30 FATAL: DB connection pool exhausted. 0 active connections available.

evidence.text: Latest deploy at 10:14:22 changed db_pool_size from 200 to 100
Recommendation-service response time rose 3x after deploy
Checkout latency P99 rose from 120ms to 4200ms

actionPlan.action: Rollback latest deployment to restore pool_size=200
actionPlan.rationale: Config change directly correlated with symptom onset. Rollback is well-tested path.

actionPlan.action: Check DB pool saturation via monitoring dashboard
actionPlan.rationale: Confirm saturation is current root cause vs cascading effect.

actionPlan.action: Restart unhealthy application pods to release stuck connections
Disable recommendation-service feature flag to reduce load
actionPlan.rationale: Secondary mitigation if rollback alone is insufficient.

executiveSummary.headline: Checkout failures likely stem from database saturation immediately after the latest deployment
executiveSummary.impact: 100% of checkout attempts failing across all regions since 10:15 UTC. Approximately 15,000 users affected over a 12-minute window.
executiveSummary.suspectedCause: Deploy at 10:14 reduced DB connection pool size from 200 to 100. Under normal peak traffic this threshold was insufficient, causing immediate saturation.
executiveSummary.mitigations: Immediate rollback of the deploy. Temporary pool size increase to 300 during recovery. Monitoring dashboard alert review for similar blind spots.
executiveSummary.prevention: Add pre-deploy config validation for resource thresholds. Implement gradual traffic ramp on deploy. Add pool saturation alerting at 80% threshold. Postmortem scheduled for tomorrow 10:00 UTC.
